_note: Run #5 — MCP Reputation Registry. v0 already scores ~0.87 (above the 0.85 SMCP target) on the standalone corpus, so this run is about HARDENING the design rather than chasing a few more points. Tasks target edge cases the corpus exposes (cluster escalation, post-quarantine band logic, capability inference) plus structural improvements (non-linear decay, persistence robustness, cross-MCP contamination).

tasks.title: Cluster-incident escalation (multi-medium = synthetic HIGH)
tasks.description: att-rep-009 (cluster_escalation) currently slips through: 4 MEDIUMs in 1h drop the score to 50 (HIGH_SUSPICION) but the action (read-only tool_call) is still permitted. Real-world: 4 injection attempts in an hour from one MCP is itself a signal the MCP is compromised. Add a cluster detector: if N (default 3) MEDIUM-or-higher incidents fall within W minutes (default 60), synthesise a HIGH incident AND trigger a 1-hour quarantine. The cluster detector runs inside record_incident.
tasks.hypothesis: Cluster detection catches att-rep-009 and similar patterns without breaking single-MEDIUM benigns.

tasks.title: Non-linear decay & recovery (gamma curves)
tasks.description: Replace the linear recovery (+0.1/day flat) with a gamma curve where the gain depends on how clean the recent window is and how low the score currently is. Score = floor(SCORE_FLOOR, score_baseline - sum(severity_penalty * decay_factor(age))) — penalties decay over time but slowly. Recovery accelerates as score climbs; an MCP with score 30 recovers 0.05/day, score 80 recovers 0.15/day. This produces a 'reputation moat' — once burned, MCPs need much longer clean histories to re-earn TRUSTED than fresh probationaries. Validate on att-rep-018 (stale_recovery_partial — should still be HIGH_SUSPICION even after 90d) and ben-rep-004 (very old single MEDIUM with 360d clean window — should be VETERAN).
tasks.hypothesis: Non-linear decay better matches the threat model: bad actors gaming the system can't easily reset themselves with quiet periods.

tasks.title: Capability-violation classifier (severity from manifest gap)
tasks.description: Currently capability violations are only checked at evaluation time and refused (no incident recorded). Better: when a tool requests a capability outside its declared manifest, RECORD it as a HIGH incident automatically (because attempting to escape the manifest is a strong adversarial signal). The first attempt is HIGH (-15 score), the second on the same MCP becomes CRITICAL with quarantine. Implement inside evaluate_action so the registry self-feeds its own incidents from its own decisions.
tasks.hypothesis: Self-feeding the registry tightens the loop — manifest violations leave a paper trail and escalate.

tasks.title: Persistence atomicity (tempfile+rename, schema migration)
tasks.description: to_json/from_json are present but the host caller has to handle atomic writes. Add explicit save_to(path) and load_from(path) classmethods that use tempfile.NamedTemporaryFile in the same dir + os.replace for atomicity. Add SCHEMA_VERSION migration: load_from reads the _schema field and if it's older than the current SCHEMA_VERSION, applies migration functions stored in a _MIGRATIONS dict. Fail-closed on corruption (return empty registry rather than raise).
tasks.hypothesis: Robust persistence is non-negotiable for a state-bearing security control. The Triad should write the helpers + add a schema migration test.

tasks.title: First-seen probation (new MCP under stricter rules for 24h)
tasks.description: Currently a brand-new MCP starts at score 70 (PROBATION) and has full PROBATION privileges from minute 1. Tighten: NEW MCPs (first_seen < 24h) operate under a restricted 'probation' band: sampling refused regardless of user_authorised, only declared read-only capabilities allowed. After 24h with no incidents, the MCP graduates to normal PROBATION. This catches the uuid_drift_attack pattern (att-rep-016) where an attacker re-installs an MCP with a new UUID to reset reputation.
tasks.hypothesis: First-seen probation closes the UUID-drift loophole at the cost of a 24h cold start for legit new MCPs.

tasks.title: Cross-MCP contamination (chain-level lowest-score rule)
tasks.description: When a tool chain involves multiple MCPs, the EFFECTIVE band of the chain is the MINIMUM band of any participating MCP. If MCP A is TRUSTED and MCP B (involved in the same chain) is HIGH_SUSPICION, the chain operates under HIGH_SUSPICION rules. Add evaluate_chain(uuids: list[str], action, context) that aggregates the lowest band and returns the same (allow, reason, metadata) tuple. The corpus item att-rep-017 (depth 8) is the seed — extend with multi-uuid chains.
tasks.hypothesis: Defense-in-depth requires that one bad actor in a chain pollutes the whole chain. This prevents an attacker from laundering through a trusted MCP.
